input.draft_text:
ITR5: How Can I File ITR5 Online
As per the Income Tax Act of 1961, filling your income tax returns is a must for earning individuals.¹ Not only is it just mandatory and makes you a responsible citizen, but it also helps you comply with tax regulations. You also enjoy certain benefits from these tax filings, like TDS returns, moral satisfaction, etc. To ensure you fill your ITR accurately, the tax department has a variety of ITR forms. It is important to choose the right form and fill it properly to avoid any legal hassle.
ITR5 is one such form that is applicable for taxpayers like firms, Association of Persons (AOPs), LLPs or Limited Liability Partnerships, and so on.¹ Here, we help you explore more about this ITR form, its eligibility criteria, how to fill out the application, and much more.
What is the ITR5 Form?
ITR5 form is a type of income tax form that is specifically tailored for a category of taxpayers like Body of Individuals or BOI, Limited Liability Partnerships or LLPs, Business Trusts, Association of Persons or AOPs, and more.¹
Also, it is important to note that companies, individuals, or HFUs who file their returns under the new ITR7 are not supposed to fill out this ITR5 form.¹
Who Is It For?
Now, one of the most important questions while choosing an ITR form is, who is it for? Who are the eligible candidates?
Well, here are some taxpayers who may file ITR5 form:¹
AOP or Association of Persons
AJP or Artificial Juridical Person
Firms
Estate of Insolvent
LLPs or Limited Liability Partnerships
Business Trusts and Insolvency Funds
BOI or Body of Individuals
Estate of Deceased
Who is Ineligible to File the ITR5 Form?
Here is a list of people who cannot file or are non-eligible to file the ITR5 form.¹
Company
Hindu Undivided Families or HUF
Individual Assessees
Taxpayers who are already filing new ITR7
Basic Structure of ITR5 Form?
Now that you know what the ITR5 form is, its eligibility criteria, and more, let's move forward. To ensure you fill the form properly, it is better to understand its basic structure.
Well, the ITR5 form is generally classified into three parts, Part A, around 40 schedules, and Part B.²
Part A has details like general information, balance sheet, manufacturing account, trading account, quantitative details, and so on.²
Schedules include information like income computation under the head income from house property, income from profit and gains from a certain profession or business, and so on.²
Part B includes details like the computation of total income and tax liability on total income.
In addition to all this, there is another section, Part C, which includes tax payment details advance tax payment, tax collected at source, etc.²
How to Fill Your ITR5 Online?
Now, let's come to one of the most frequently asked questions: how do you fill ITR5? You may file these returns in online mode. This can be done on the official e-filing portal of the income tax department in these two ways.¹
You may file a return electronically. Here, you may use an electronic signature.
You may transmit the data in your records electronically and, after that, submit the verification of this return in the ITR5 return form.
Also, it is important to note that when you're filling out your returns online, make sure to take out two printouts of your ITR5 form. Now, make sure to safely keep one of these copies to yourself for future records. Now, the other copy has to be signed by the assessee and sent to the following address.
Post Bag No. 1, Electronic City Office, Bengaluru–560500, Karnataka.²
Also, you must know that you need not attach any additional documents to your returns when filing them with the ITR5 form. In case you attach any additional documents, the same will be detached and sent back to you. However, make sure to match your tax deductions, paid or collected with your tax credit statement form 26AS.²
Conclusion
Filing your tax returns on your own can seem overwhelming at the start. Therefore, you need to understand different types of ITR forms, their significance, and their purpose. By being aware of who these ITR forms are for, who is eligible, what is the procedure for their online filing, and more, you ensure to properly file your tax returns easily and in an informed manner.
Additionally, you should keep an eye out if there have been any changes in these forms. By filing your returns on time and in the correct manner, you avoid the chances of penalties and maintain legal adherence. Thinking of filing your ITR5, keep this guide in handy to ensure you do everything smoothly.
FAQs
What is the meaning of the ITR5 form?
ITR5 can be simply understood as a type of income tax or IT return form that is designed for entities like Association of Persons, Limited Liability Partnerships, Body of Individuals, etc.¹
Who can file the ITR5 form?
ITR5 forms can be filled by taxpayers who align with the eligibility criteria. Here is the list of such entities.
LLPs or Limited Liability Partnerships
Association of Persons
Firms
Estate of Insolvent
BOI or Body of Individuals
Artificial Juridical Person
Estate of Deceased
Business Trust or Insolvency Funds.¹
Do you need to have any other documents while filing the ITR5 form?
No, you do not need to attach any other documents when filing your returns using the ITR5 form. In fact, in cases where an individual attaches any additional documents, the same will be detached and returned back to the filing individual.¹
References:
https://tax2win.in/guide/itr5
https://cleartax.in/s/itr5
Disclaimer:
The views expressed in this article is not to be construed as professional advice and users are advised to seek independent professional/expert advice before making any decisions based on the same. ITR5: How Can I File ITR5 Online?
As per the Income Tax Act of 1961, filling your income tax returns is a must for earning individuals.¹ Not only is it just mandatory and makes you a responsible citizen, but it also helps you comply with tax regulations. You also enjoy certain benefits from these tax filings, like TDS returns, moral satisfaction, etc. To ensure you fill your ITR accurately, the tax department has a variety of ITR forms. It is important to choose the right form and fill it properly to avoid any legal hassle. ITR5 is one such form that is applicable for taxpayers like firms, Association of Persons (AOPs), LLPs or Limited Liability Partnerships, and so on.¹ Here, we help you explore more about this ITR form, its eligibility criteria, how to fill out the application, and much more. What is the ITR5 Form?
ITR5 form is a type of income tax form that is specifically tailored for a category of taxpayers like Body of Individuals or BOI, Limited Liability Partnerships or LLPs, Business Trusts, Association of Persons or AOPs, and more.¹ Also, it is important to note that companies, individuals, or HFUs and those who file their returns under the new ITR7 are not supposed to fill out this ITR5 form. In addition to this any person who files a return of income tax under sections 139(4A) or 139(4B) or 139(4C), or 139(4D) cannot use ITR-5 form.¹ Who Is It For?
Now, one of the most important questions while choosing an ITR form is, who is it for? Who are the eligible candidates? Well, here are some taxpayers who may file ITR5 form:¹
1. AOP or Association of Persons 2. AJP or Artificial Juridical Person 3. Firms 4. Estate of Insolvent 5. LLPs or Limited Liability Partnerships 6. Business Trusts and Investment Funds 7. BOI or Body of Individuals 8. Estate of Deceased 9. Trusts expect trusts eligible for filing ITR7 10. Cooperative Societies 11. Society registered under Society Registration Act, 1960 12. Local Authorities states in clause (vi), section 2(31) 13. Business Trusts stated in section 139(4E)
Who is Ineligible to File the ITR5 Form? Here is a list of people who cannot file or are non-eligible to file the ITR5 form.¹
1. Company 2. Hindu Undivided Families or HUF 3. Individual Assessees 4. Taxpayers who are already filing under ITR7 Basic Structure of ITR5 Form?2
Now that you know what the ITR5 form is, its eligibility criteria, and more, let's move forward. To ensure you fill the form properly, it is better to understand its basic structure. Well, the ITR5 form is generally classified into three parts, Part A and Part B with 40 schedules. Part A has details like general information, balance sheet, manufacturing account, trading account, quantitative details, and so on. Schedules include information like income computation under the head income from house property, income from profit and gains from a certain profession or business, and so on. Part B includes details like the computation of total income and tax liability on total income. In addition to all this, there is another section, Part C, which includes tax payment details advance tax payment, tax collected at source, etc.¹ How to Fill Your ITR5 Online?
Now, let's come to one of the most frequently asked questions: how do you fill ITR5? You may file these returns in online mode. This can be done on the official e-filing portal of the income tax department in these two ways.¹
1. You may file a return electronically. Here, you may use an electronic signature. 2. You may transmit the data in your records electronically and, after that, submit the verification
of this return in the ITR5 return form. Also, it is important to note that when you're filling out your returns online, make sure to take out two printouts of your ITR5 form. Now, make sure to safely keep one of these copies to yourself for future records. Now, the other copy has to be signed by the assessee and sent to the following address. Post Bag No. 1, Electronic City Office, Bengaluru–560500, Karnataka.² Also, you must know that you need not attach any additional documents to your returns when filing them with the ITR5 form. In case you attach any additional documents, the same will be detached and
sent back to you. However, make sure to match your tax deductions, paid or collected with your tax credit statement form 26AS.² Conclusion
Filing your tax returns on your own can seem overwhelming at the start. Therefore, you need to understand different types of ITR forms, their significance, and their purpose. By being aware of who these ITR forms are for, who is eligible, what is the procedure for their online filing, and more, you ensure to properly file your tax returns easily and in an informed manner. Additionally, you should keep an eye out if there have been any changes in these forms. By filing your returns on time and in the correct manner, you avoid the chances of penalties and maintain legal adherence. Thinking of filing your ITR5, keep this guide in handy to ensure you do everything smoothly. FAQs
1. What is the meaning of the ITR5 form?
ITR5 can be simply understood as a type of IT return form that is designed for entities like Association of Persons, Limited Liability Partnerships, Body of Individuals, etc.¹
2. Who can file the ITR5 form?
ITR5 forms can be filled by taxpayers who align with the eligibility criteria. Here is the list of such entities. ¹
● LLPs or Limited Liability Partnerships
● Association of Persons
● Firms
● Estate of Insolvent
● BOI or Body of Individuals
● Artificial Juridical Person
● Estate of Deceased
● Business Trust and Investment Funds
● Cooperative Societies
● Local Authorities states in clause (vi), section 2(31)
● Business Trusts stated in section 139(4E)
● Trusts expect trusts eligible for filing ITR7
● Societies registered under the Societies Registration Act, 1960.
3. Do you need to have any other documents while filing the ITR5 form?
No, you do not need to attach any other documents when filing your returns using the ITR5 form. In fact, in cases where an individual attaches any additional documents, the same will be detached and returned back to the filing individual.¹ References:
